beats.activity: Wake up early, check on balcony plants and seedlings despite the drizzle, make coffee.
beats.reasoning: Hiro is an early riser who tends to plants first thing; the rain won't stop him from a quick check on his tomatoes and seedlings.

beats.activity: Commute to Kirkland Public Library, taking 520 westbound to avoid the eastbound closure and detour via I-90 and surface streets.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so Hiro leaves early (before 9am) to cross via westbound 520 without delay, arriving well before his shift.

beats.activity: Work shift: shelving returns, reference desk, helping patrons with research and tech questions.
beats.reasoning: Standard mid-morning work block before lunch; the steady rain outside keeps the library moderately busy with indoor activity.

beats.activity: Lunch break at the library: eat a sandwich at the break room, read a chapter of a book, quiet time.
beats.reasoning: Consistent with yesterday's pattern of eating lunch at the desk/break room; the rain makes venturing out unappealing.

beats.activity: Work shift: afternoon reference desk, cataloging, helping with library programs.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block covers the tail end of the rain window (rain ends around 2pm); standard librarian duties.

beats.activity: Commute home to Mercer Island, taking 520 westbound back across the lake (closure is now lifted).
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure ends at noon, so the return commute is clear; Hiro heads home as the rain clears.

beats.activity: Tend to the garden: water beds, check seedlings after the rain, deadhead any spent blooms.
beats.reasoning: Rain has cleared by late afternoon; Hiro takes advantage of the cooler, damp evening to garden and check on his plants.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, sit down to a proper meal while reviewing vinyl collection.
beats.reasoning: Following yesterday's pattern of making a proper dinner; pairs it with his vinyl hobby for a calm evening.

beats.activity: Hand-write letters and journal entries, listen to vinyl records.
beats.reasoning: Hiro values quiet evenings for correspondence and reflection; low-energy mood (60/100) and drizzly weather support staying in and enjoying vinyl.

diary: Woke early to check on my seedlings despite the drizzle—they're really taking off this season. The commute was smooth enough; I'd heard about the 520 closure and took the westbound route, which saved me from the real mess everyone else was dealing with. Work was steady, the kind of Tuesday where I helped a woman track down some obscure Seattle history books and walked an older gentleman through using the library's new digital catalog system. There's something satisfying about that. Grabbed my sandwich in the break room and just sat with my book for a bit—needed the quiet. By evening the rain had stopped, so I spent time in the garden, checking on everything. The seedlings loved the moisture. Made a proper dinner, put on some Coltrane, and spent the night hand-writing letters I've been meaning to send. Felt grounded today, like I was exactly where I needed to be.
thought_process: The grey weather actually settled me rather than dragging me down—I think because it gave me permission to move slowly. I intentionally chose the westbound route on 520 knowing the eastbound closure would be chaos; small planning like that pays off. Work felt purposeful today, especially helping patrons navigate both physical and digital spaces. My mood stayed even because I built in those quiet moments: the lunch break, the evening garden time. I didn't push myself. The vinyl and letter-writing felt like natural extensions of that pace rather than obligations. Sometimes Tuesdays need to be gentle.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
